reason: Both correctly identify exactly-once delivery as impossible (Two Generals problem/ack ambiguity) and correctly distinguish delivery vs. processing, landing on at-least-once + idempotency as the standard. Both are concise and well-organized. However, Answer B includes an additional key mechanism — transactional processing (atomically committing offset/ack with side effects, e.g., outbox pattern) — which is a standard and important part of the real-world implementation of exactly-once processing that Answer A omits (A only covers ID-based dedup). B also cites a broader range of real systems (Kafka EOS, Flink, SQS FIFO). Both correctly caveat that external side effects still need separate idempotency handling. B is marginally more complete without added length or padding.
